# @zakkster/lite-headless / separator

A thin divider between content groups. Semantic by default (announced as a
group boundary) or decorative (visual only, hidden from assistive tech).

## Quick example

```js
import { createSeparator } from "@zakkster/lite-headless/separator";

const s = createSeparator({ orientation: "horizontal" });
s.attachRoot(hrEl);

s.setOrientation("vertical");   // reactive; repaints data-orientation + aria
```

Decorative (no role, hidden from screen readers):
```js
createSeparator({ decorative: true });
```

## Options

| Option | Default | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `orientation` | `"horizontal"` | `horizontal \| vertical` |
| `decorative` | `false` | `true` -> role="none" + aria-hidden (visual only) |

## API

```ts
{
    orientation(): "horizontal" | "vertical"
    isDecorative: boolean                 // readonly
    setOrientation(o): void

    attachRoot(el): off
    destroy(): void
}
```

## Painted attributes

- `data-separator-root` -- slot marker
- `data-orientation="horizontal|vertical"` -- Class 3 enum, always present
- `role="separator"` -- semantic mode (only if you didn't pre-set a role)
- `aria-orientation="vertical"` -- semantic mode, vertical only (horizontal is the ARIA default)
- `role="none"` + `aria-hidden="true"` -- decorative mode

## CSS contract

`data-orientation` is a Class 3 enum; `data-separator-root` is a Class 4 slot
marker. The primitive paints no size -- the consumer draws the rule:

```css
[data-separator-root] { background: var(--border); }
[data-separator-root][data-orientation="horizontal"] { block-size: 1px; inline-size: 100%; }
[data-separator-root][data-orientation="vertical"]   { inline-size: 1px; block-size: 100%; }
```

## Wrapper (`<lite-separator>`)

```html
<lite-separator></lite-separator>
<lite-separator orientation="vertical"></lite-separator>
<lite-separator decorative></lite-separator>
```

Reactive: the `orientation` attribute flows through bidirectionally.
Host: `setOrientation(o)`, `orientation` getter.
